1.description: Value of cash management: wholesale market. The figure depicts the wholesale market, in which suppliers have perfectly elastic supply (horizontal lines) and dispensaries have imperfectly elastic demand (diagonal line). Quantity (in grams) is on the horizontal axis and price is on the vertical axis. Operating without cash management (CM) shifts the supply curve upward, from “Supply with CM” to “Supply without CM.” [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

2.description: Value of cash management: retail market. The figure depicts the retail market, in which customers have perfectly elastic demand (horizontal lines) and dispensaries have imperfectly elastic supply (diagonal line). Quantity (in grams) is on the horizontal axis and price is on the vertical axis. Operating without cash management (CM) shifts the demand curve downward, from “Demand with CM” to “Demand without CM.” [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

3.description: Marijuana dispensaries in Washington. The figure shows the locations of marijuana dispensaries across counties in Washington state. These retail establishments are located in 152 cities or towns within the state. Counties are shaded from light to dark based on county-level marijuana sales (in grams). The lightest shade represents counties with the least sales while the darkest shade represents counties with the most sales. Dots represent a marijuana dispensary. The data used to construct the map come from the Washington State Liquor and Cannabis Board. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

4.description: Marijuana profit by county and location of credit unions. The figure shows the locations of credit unions across counties in Washington state. Credit unions that work with marijuana dispensaries are denoted by dark dots, while lighter dots correspond to non-502 credit unions. Counties are shaded from light to dark based on county-level marijuana dispensary profitability. The lightest shade represents counties with the lowest profitability and the darkest represents counties with the highest profitability. The data used to construct this map come from the Washington State Liquor and Cannabis Board and the National Credit Union Administration. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

5.description: Timeline of events. The figure presents the time series of events related to the legalization of recreational marijuana in Washington state. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

6.description: Log profitability and distance to financial institutions. The figure plots log(Profitability) at a dispensary by strain (vertical axis) by the dispensary's distance to a financial institution (horizontal axis).Distance to 502 credit unionis depicted by a solid line,Distance to non-502 credit unionby a dashed line, andDistance to bankby a dotted line.Profitabilityis measured at the transaction strain level asProfitability= (retail price − wholesale price)×$\times$quantity. Distance is calculated as straight-line distance. Credit unions that work with the marijuana industry are defined as 502 credit unions. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

7.description: Placebo tests of profitability and banking services. The figure summarizes results of placebo tests of log(Profitability) usingDistance to non-502 credit unionorDistance to nearest bankas alternative instrumental variables. We provide three sets of estimates. The first usesDistance to non-502 credit union(“credit union”). The second and third sets of results useDistance to nearest bankin two different samples: the second set uses the same sample as our main results (“Bank (credit union sample)”), restricting attention to within 10 miles of a 502 credit union, while the third set uses a different restriction (“Bank”), that restricts attention to within 10 miles of a bank. For each set of tests, we report estimates with strain fixed effects, week and strain fixed effects, city and strain fixed effects, and city, week, and strain fixed effects. The bars represent 95% confidence intervals. Standard errors are clustered at the dispensary level. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

8.description: Sensitivity tests of profitability and cash management. The figure summarizes results of sensitivity tests of howProfitabilitychanges with access to cash management along with 95% confidence intervals. Standard errors are clustered at the dispensary level. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
